turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I waited at the restaurant for forty minutes, Leo. I kept checking my phone, and I felt embarrassed sitting there alone.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I know. I should have texted. My meeting ran over and then Alex pulled me into that budget call.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: You always have a reason. It is never just, "I forgot you."

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: That is not fair. I didn't forget you. I was dealing with work.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: It felt like you forgot me.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Okay, but that's different from saying I did. I don't like being accused of not caring.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I'm not trying to accuse you. I'm trying to explain that I felt like I didn't matter.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I hear that, but when you say "you always" and "you never," it makes me feel like there is no point in explaining.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Maybe because the explanations come after the damage is already done.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: What was I supposed to do? Walk out of a meeting with the CFO?

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Send one text. "Running late." That is all.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I agree I could have done that.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: You say that now, but this keeps happening. Last month it was my cousin's birthday, and before that it was the airport pickup.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: The airport pickup was different. Your flight landed early.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: See, this is what I mean. You turn every example into a technicality.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Because the details matter. I feel like I'm being put on trial.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I feel like I have to build a case for you to take me seriously.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I do take you seriously.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Then why do I have to get this upset before you listen?

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I don't know. Maybe because when you start with "you always," I get defensive immediately.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I can own that. I was angry, and I said it too broadly.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Thank you. I also own that I should have texted. I messed up there.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I appreciate you saying that.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I don't want you sitting somewhere feeling abandoned.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: That's the word. Abandoned. I know it sounds dramatic, but that's what it felt like.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: It doesn't sound dramatic. I can see how sitting there alone would feel awful.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: But I also need you to understand that when work explodes, I'm not choosing work over you. Sometimes I'm just trying not to drop a ball.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I can understand that. I don't need instant replies all day. I need to know when plans change.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: That seems reasonable.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: And I need you not to make me feel silly for being upset.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I didn't mean to. When I said it was "just dinner," that was dismissive.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Yeah. That hurt.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I'm sorry. It wasn't just dinner to you. It was me not showing up.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Exactly.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Could we agree that if either of us is going to be more than fifteen minutes late, we text?

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Yes. And if you truly can't text, then call as soon as you can and say what happened without making me drag it out.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I can do that.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I also need to work on not jumping straight to "you don't care."

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: That would help. Because I do care.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I know you do. I just don't always feel it in those moments.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Maybe when that happens, you can say, "I'm starting to feel unimportant," instead of "you never care."

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: That sounds fair. I might not say it perfectly when I'm angry.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: I don't need perfect. I just need a chance to hear you before I'm already the villain.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: And I need a chance to be hurt without you immediately defending yourself.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Okay. So, text if late, acknowledge first, explain second?

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: Yes. And I will try to use less absolute language.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Deal.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I'm still upset, but I feel better than I did ten minutes ago.

turns.speaker: Leo
turns.text: Me too. I love you.

turns.speaker: Maya
turns.text: I love you too.
